explanations.1: Frequency is determined by the source of the oscillation and does not change when the wave enters a different medium.
explanations.2: The wavelength in a medium is calculated by dividing the vacuum wavelength by the index of refraction ($600 \text{ nm} / 1.33 = 451 \text{ nm}$).
explanations.3: Geometric optics (ray modeling) is valid when the wavelength of light is negligible compared to the size of the objects it encounters.
explanations.4: Huygens’s principle states that every point on a wavefront acts as a source of wavelets that spread out in the forward direction.
explanations.5: Diffraction effects are most noticeable when the wavelength of the wave is on the same order of magnitude as the opening it passes through.
explanations.6: Coherence is necessary for a stable interference pattern and means the waves maintain a consistent phase relative to each other.
explanations.7: Constructive interference occurs when the path length difference between the two slits is an integral multiple of the wavelength.
explanations.8: According to the equation $\sin \theta = m\lambda/d$, as the slit separation $d$ decreases, the angle $\theta$ for a given order $m$ must increase.
explanations.10: A large number of slits results in more precise constructive interference, making the bright regions narrower and easier to measure.
explanations.11: The slit spacing $d$ is the reciprocal of the number of lines per unit length ($1 / 500,000\text{ lines/m} = 2 \times 10^{-6}\text{ m}$).
explanations.12: Red light has the longest wavelength in the visible spectrum, and the angle of diffraction increases with wavelength.
explanations.13: Single slit diffraction is characterized by a central peak that is twice as wide and significantly more intense than the subsequent peaks.
explanations.14: Destructive interference for a single slit first occurs when the path difference between the top and bottom of the slit is exactly one wavelength.
explanations.16: This specific condition provides a standard for determining when two closely spaced point sources can just be distinguished.
explanations.18: Diffraction limits the detail of an image to approximately the wavelength of the radiation used to probe the object.
explanations.19: Reflection from a more optically dense medium results in a $180^\circ$ (or $\lambda/2$) phase shift.
explanations.20: At near-zero thickness, the only factor is the $180^\circ$ phase shift from the first surface, resulting in the two rays being out of phase.
explanations.21: Since shifts occur at both interfaces, for destructive interference $2t = \lambda_n / 2$, so $t = \lambda / (4n) = 552 / (4 \times 1.38) = 100 \text{ nm}$.
explanations.22: By convention, the polarization of light refers to the orientation of the electric field vector.
explanations.23: According to Malus’s Law, $I = I_0 \cos^2(60^\circ) = I_0 (0.5)^2 = 0.25 I_0$.
explanations.24: Brewster’s angle is found using $\tan \theta_b = n_2 / n_1 = 1.33 / 1.00$, which gives $\theta_b = 53.1^\circ$.
explanations.25: Glare is mostly horizontally polarized light reflected from surfaces, which is blocked by the vertical transmission axes of Polaroid lenses.
explanations.26: Optical activity is the property of certain substances, like sugar solutions, to rotate the plane of polarized light.
explanations.27: It converts variations in path length and phase into variations in intensity, making transparent structures visible.
explanations.28: Confocal microscopy uses spatial filtering (pinholes) to exclude light from planes outside the focal point.
explanations.30: The circular interference pattern reveals the gap thickness between a lens and a surface, allowing for sub-wavelength accuracy in shape comparison.
